name: boF20ErrorHandling
description:
Short description: Error Handling build operator. Long description: Error Handling build operator

Version Number: 1.0
Date: 26/01/2009
Author: John Campbell         

Description
------------------

This operator takes input data consisting of the error handling columns: pscErrorCode and pscErrorInfo plus RCP data fed into the error handling shared container.

The supplied error code is used to look up an in-memory copy of the "errors.csv" (converted to null-separated values) format by the job JP_F20_CreateErrorsNSV_010 as a memory-mapped file, to get user-defined error categorisations/description.

The input and error data is augmented by standard DataStage macro and other log data (current timestamp) and is appended to an error log file named according to the framework standards:

   RunId.HostName.ProjectName.JobName.InvocationId.StageName.YYYYMMDDHHMMSS.err

If the supplied error code is defined as severity 1, the operator will abort the job once the log is written.

If the supplied error code does not exist or is empty, the operator will contrive an error code, DS_ERR_0 that is severity 1. Such a scenario will result in the job to stop processing and fail.

Please check the details in error handling shared container for further information.

NOTE 1: The C++ functions can not be called twice within a statement. For this reason many statements have been split into several lines.

NOTE 2: The RCP data columns may appear in a different order within the log file, and this happens if their names have been automatically generated (by appending _1, _2, etc) within the transformer stage.

properties.description: Directory containing the file with the list of all error codes and additional information
Directory that will contain the error files that are created by every job
Yes or No determines if RCP data is written to the error log
properties.prompt: Include RCP data in error log

properties.description: Number of warnings to log. 0 to do not log any warnings. -1 to log all warnings, otherwise write number of warning specified
